full_response:
We want to construct, from an arbitrary rank-2 tensor T_{ab}, a new tensor T_{[ab]} that  

1. is antisymmetric: T_{[ab]} = -T_{[ba]},  
2. reduces to T_{ab} itself when T_{ab} is already antisymmetric.

The unique expression that satisfies those two requirements is obtained by subtracting the “transpose” part of the tensor and dividing by two (the factor ½ ensures property 2).  Explicitly,

Step-by-step derivation  
1. Start with T_{ab}.  
2. Form the quantity T_{ab} - T_{ba}; this is automatically antisymmetric because  
 (T_{ab} - T_{ba})_{ba} = T_{ba} - T_{ab} = -(T_{ab} - T_{ba}).  
3. To ensure that, if T_{ab} is already antisymmetric (T_{ab} = -T_{ba}), the new object equals T_{ab}, insert a factor ½:  
 If T_{ab} = -T_{ba}, then ½(T_{ab} - T_{ba}) = ½(T_{ab} + T_{ab}) = T_{ab}.  

Hence

T_{[ab]} = ½ (T_{ab} − T_{ba}).  

This tensor is manifestly antisymmetric and meets the required condition.

\boxed{T_{[ab]} = \dfrac{1}{2}\bigl(T_{ab} - T_{ba}\bigr)}

Confidence: 96%
